title: Baseline by Second Son Consulting (com.secondsonconsulting.baseline)
description: Baseline by Second Son Consulting Configuration

DisplayName.title: Display Name
DisplayName.description: The name you want to appear in SwiftDialog and logs. Initial Scripts will only be shown to the user if the item Fails.

ScriptPath.title: Script Path
ScriptPath.description: A path to the script you want to run. Can be a local file path or a URL.

SHA256.title: SHA256
SHA256.description: The expected SHA256 of the script being run.

MD5.title: MD5
MD5.description: The expected MD5 of the script being run.

Arguments.title: Arguments
Arguments.description: Arguments you want to pass to the script when it is run.

AsUser.title: Run as User
AsUser.description: Enable this option to run the script as the logged in user.

Retries.title: Retries
Retries.description: The number of times Baseline will retry this specific item if it fails. If omitted, will use the Global Retries Default.

CurlOptions.title: Curl Options
CurlOptions.description: Custom Curl Options to used when downloading this script.

InitialScripts.title: InitialScripts
InitialScripts.description: Define scripts that are run immediately as the user logs in, prior to any Baseline SwiftDialog windows. These scripts are the place to add your custom welcome messages or pre-flight scripts.

DisplayName.title: Display Name
DisplayName.description: The name you want to appear in the SwiftDialog menu as this Installomator label is processed.

Arguments.title: Arguments
Arguments.description: Arguments you want to pass to the Installomator command for this label.

Icon.title: Icon
Icon.description: The icon you want to appear in the SwiftDialog menu as this Installomator label is processed. Can be a URL or local file path.

Subtitle.title: Subtitle
Subtitle.description: The subtitle text to appear on this line item.

StatusIconWait.title: Wait Icon
StatusIconWait.description: The icon used for the wait list status, for this specific line item.

StatusIconSuccess.title: Success Icon
StatusIconSuccess.description: The icon used for the success list status, for this specific line item.

StatusIconFail.title: Fail Icon
StatusIconFail.description: The icon used for the fail list status, for this specific line item.

Retries.title: Retries
Retries.description: The number of times Baseline will retry this specific item if it fails. If omitted, will use the Global Retries Default.

HideListView.title: Hide List While Running
HideListView.description: Hide the window while this item is running. Default is false

Installomator.title: Installomator Labels
Installomator.description: Define Installomator labels to be run by Baseline. For information specific to Installomator, see the Installomator Github: https://github.com/Installomator/Installomator

DisplayName.title: Display Name
DisplayName.description: The name you want to appear in the SwiftDialog menu for this item we are waiting for.

Path.title: Path
Path.description: A path to the file you want Baseline to wait for.

Icon.title: Icon
Icon.description: The icon you want to appear in the SwiftDialog menu for this item. Can be a URL or local file path.

Subtitle.title: Subtitle
Subtitle.description: The subtitle text to appear on this line item.

WaitFor.title: WaitFor
WaitFor.description: Define files which you want Baseline to wait for. Use this for items not directly installed by Baseline, like VPP or MDM installed apps.

DisplayName.title: Display Name
DisplayName.description: The name you want to appear SwiftDialog and logs. Final Scripts will only be shown to the user if the item Fails.

ScriptPath.title: Script Path
ScriptPath.description: A path to the script you want to run. Can be a local file path or a URL.

SHA256.title: SHA256
SHA256.description: The expected SHA256 of the script being run.

MD5.title: MD5
MD5.description: The expected MD5 of the script being run.

Arguments.title: Arguments
Arguments.description: Arguments you want to pass to the script when it is run.

AsUser.title: Run as User
AsUser.description: Enable this option to run the script as the logged in user.

Retries.title: Retries
Retries.description: The number of times Baseline will retry this specific item if it fails. If omitted, will use the Global Retries Default.

CurlOptions.title: Curl Options
CurlOptions.description: Custom Curl Options to used when downloading this script.

FinalScripts.title: FinalScripts
FinalScripts.description: Define scripts that are run just before completion dialogs. For reporting webhooks, cleanup tasks, etc. Similar to InitialScripts, these items do not appear on the List View.

WaitForTimeout.title: WaitFor Timeout
WaitForTimeout.description: When using WaitFor, enter the number of seconds you wish to wait before Baseline considers remaining items a failure. This timer does not start until all other items are processed. Default if key is omitted is 600.

Restart.title: Force Restart
Restart.description: This setting controls whether Baseline forces a restart after completion.

LogOut.title: Force Log Out
LogOut.description: This setting controls whether Baseline forces a restart after completion.

CleanupAfterUse.title: Cleanup After Use
CleanupAfterUse.description: This setting controls whether Baseline deletes it's install directory after use: /usr/local/Baseline.

IgnoreDefaultInstallomatorOptions.title: Ignore Default Installomator Options
IgnoreDefaultInstallomatorOptions.description: By default, Baseline uses "NOTIFY=silent" and "BLOCKING_PROCESS_ACTION=kill" Installomator options. Setting this to True means Baseline will not use those options.

SkipSuccessDialog.title: Skip Success Dialog
SkipSuccessDialog.description: If set to true, Baseline will not present a Success dialog at completion.

SkipFailDialog.title: Skip Failure Dialog
SkipFailDialog.description: If set to true, Baseline will not present a Failure dialog at completion.

SilentMode.title: Silent Mode
SilentMode.description: If set to true, no dialog windows will be created. All items and item types will process as normal.

CloseListBeforeFinalScripts.title: Close List View Before Final Scripts
CloseListBeforeFinalScripts.description: If set to true, Baseline will close the list view window before running FinalScripts. Use this option if you want to utilize FinalScripts to have the user complete actions prior to the Baseline completion dialog.

DialogListOptions.title: Dialog List Window Options
DialogListOptions.description: SwiftDialog options for the primary Baseline progress list window.

DialogSuccessOptions.title: Dialog Success Window Options
DialogSuccessOptions.description: SwiftDialog options for the Success Dialog window.

DialogFailureOptions.title: Dialog Failure Window Options
DialogFailureOptions.description: SwiftDialog options for the Failure Dialog window.

DialogInspectModeOptions.title: Dialog Inspect Mode Window Options
DialogInspectModeOptions.description: SwiftDialog options for Inspect Mode. Most options should be in the JSON file, but some can be passed here. See swiftDialog documentation for specifics.

InspectModeJSON.title: Inspect Mode JSON
InspectModeJSON.description: Path to your Inspect Mode JSON file. Using this option enables "Inspect Mode". DialogListView options will not be respected, and no Baseline DialogListView will be drawn. Inspect Mode is enabled after InitialScripts

BlurScreen.title: Blur Screen
BlurScreen.description: This setting controls whether Baseline uses the SwiftDialog --blurscreen feature. Default is true. If you set to false, consider using --ontop in your DialogListOptions key

ShowList.title: Show List
ShowList.description: If false, the ListView will not show any list items. Instead, a standard Dialog window is drawn with the options configured in `DialogListOptions`. If used with progress bar, the progress bar will still be displayed.

ProgressBar.title: Show Progress Bar
ProgressBar.description: This setting controls whether Baseline displays a progress bar.

ProgressBarDisplayNames.title: Show Display Names on Progress Bar
ProgressBarDisplayNames.description: This setting controls whether Baseline displays the current item being processed under the progress bar.

InstallomatorSwiftDialogIntegration.title: Use Installomator SwiftDialog Integration
InstallomatorSwiftDialogIntegration.description: Tell Installomator to use it's built in SwiftDialog integration for the List View.

JamfVerbose.title: Verbose Jamf Items
JamfVerbose.description: If set to true, when a Script is processing a jamf policy (/usr/local/bin/jamf), Baseline will watch the Jamf log to provide more verbose output on that line item. Similar to the Installomator integration, text and a progress circle will be used for all jamf items.

Button1Enabled.title: Button 1 Enabled
Button1Enabled.description: This setting enables SwiftDialog "Button 1" (the OK button) on the Baseline List View. This allows users to dismiss the list view and continue working while Baseline runs. By default this button is disabled.

GlobalDialogStatusIconWait.title: Global Status Icon - Wait
GlobalDialogStatusIconWait.description: The icon used for the wait list status, if no specific wait status icon is set.

GlobalDialogStatusIconSuccess.title: Global Status Icon - Success
GlobalDialogStatusIconSuccess.description: The icon used for the success list status, if no specific success status icon is set.

GlobalDialogStatusIconFail.title: Global Status Icon - Fail
GlobalDialogStatusIconFail.description: The icon used for the fail list status, if no specific fail status icon is set.

PendingWaitForIcon.title: Wait For Pending Icon
PendingWaitForIcon.description: The icon used for the "pending" status of all WaitFor items. Leave blank for nothing. Use "pending" for swiftDialog Default.

ReinstallDialog.title: Reinstall SwiftDialog
ReinstallDialog.description: Deprecated: This feature was removed in v.2.3, as it is no longer needed. This setting controls whether Baseline will reinstall SwiftDialog as the final step before completion. Default behavior if this key is omitted will reinstall SwiftDialog if a custom PNG is found in /Library/Application Support/Dialog/Dialog.png. Leave this as default if you use Baseline to deploy a custom SwiftDialog icon. Set to false if you do not want Baseline to ever reinstall SwiftDialog. Set to True if you always want to reinstall SwiftDialog after each Baseline.

ExitCondition.title: Exit Condition
ExitCondition.description: Define a file or folder path. When Baseline starts, if this file or folder exists, Baseline will exit quietly without processing any items. Use this option if your management tool may re-send scripts when not desired.

BailOutFile.title: Bail Out File
BailOutFile.description: Define a file path. If this file exists at any point while Baseline is running, Baseline will exit and will not process additional items.

DefaultInstallomatorRetries.title: Default Installomator Retries
DefaultInstallomatorRetries.description: Default number of retries for Installomator items. Default 0

DefaultScriptRetries.title: Default Script Retries
DefaultScriptRetries.description: Default number of retries for Script items. Default 0

DefaultPackageRetries.title: Default Package Retries
DefaultPackageRetries.description: Default number of retries for Package items. Default 0
